Title: The jasmonate-induced bHLH gene SIJIG functions in terpene biosynthesis and resistance to insects and fungus

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against herbivorous insects and fungal pathogen; reduced attraction and growth of Helicoverpa armigera and enhanced resistance to Botrytis cinerea
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript levels in leaves are reduced in sijig mutants and show weaker MeJA induction than wild type
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is positively regulated by SIJIG and contributes to terpene accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS5 transcript abundance was lower in sijig mutants than in wild type. 2. JA-mediated induction of TPS5 was weaker in sijig mutants. 3. Reduced TPS5 expression is associated with lower leaf terpene contents in sijig mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq-supported differential expression; GC-MS metabolite profiling at pathway level
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway output gene because it was specifically validated among TPS genes in Results; direct catalytic product was not characterized in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against herbivorous insects and fungal pathogen
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript levels in leaves are reduced in sijig mutants and show weaker MeJA induction than wild type
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is positively regulated by SIJIG and contributes to terpene accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS9 was downregulated in sijig mutants. 2. MeJA-induced TPS9 expression was attenuated in sijig mutants. 3. Its reduced expression is part of the lowered terpene biosynthetic program in sijig mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq-supported differential expression; GC-MS metabolite profiling at pathway level
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Validated TPS family member highlighted in Results; no direct enzymatic assay reported in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against herbivorous insects and fungal pathogen
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript levels in leaves are reduced in sijig mutants and show weaker MeJA induction than wild type
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is positively regulated by SIJIG and contributes to terpene accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS12 expression decreased in sijig mutants. 2. JA-triggered induction of TPS12 was compromised in sijig mutants. 3. Lower TPS12 expression accompanies reduced terpene contents in mutant leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq-supported differential expression; GC-MS metabolite profiling at pathway level
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Validated downstream pathway gene; specific product assignment not given in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against herbivorous insects and fungal pathogen
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript levels in leaves are reduced in sijig mutants and show weaker MeJA induction than wild type
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is positively regulated by SIJIG and contributes to terpene accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS20 transcript levels were lower in sijig mutants than in wild type. 2. MeJA responsiveness of TPS20 was weakened in sijig mutants. 3. This supports SIJIG-dependent activation of terpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq-supported differential expression; GC-MS metabolite profiling at pathway level
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Validated downstream TPS gene in Results; no direct promoter binding by SIJIG shown.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related volatile terpenes that reduce attraction and growth of Helicoverpa armigera and contribute to resistance against Botrytis cinerea
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Terpene biosynthesis and JA-mediated defense responses
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: JA-induced downstream MYC2 target required for full accumulation of leaf terpenes and for proper induction of terpene synthase genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation, likely direct or indirect activation of TPS genes; acts downstream of MYC2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively affects expression of TPS5; TPS9; TPS12; TPS20 and defense genes PI-II; TD; PR-STH2; JA2L under inducing conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout lowers leaf terpene contents, weakens JA-induced TPS expression, increases attractiveness to cotton bollworm, increases larval weight gain, and reduces resistance to Botrytis cinerea
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by MeJA within 1 h in a COI1-dependent manner; induced by Botrytis cinerea; highest basal expression reported in flower buds and small fruits by eFP browser
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong MeJA inducibility in leaves; induced by B. cinerea infection
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment; wounding; Botrytis cinerea infection; COI1-dependent JA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a functional MYC2-SIJIG transcriptional module; no interaction detected with JAZ proteins, MYC1, EOT1, JA2L, or ERF.3C in tested assays
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate/MeJA; COI1-mediated JA perception; MYC2; Botrytis cinerea infection
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout constructs for functional analysis; endogenous promoter for native regulation; pJIG:LUC reporter used in dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to enhance terpene-mediated insect and fungal resistance in tomato, potentially via activation or allele engineering in JA-responsive contexts; caution that direct targets and interacting partners remain unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; GC-MS; insect feeding assays; fungal infection assays; subcellular localization; Y2H/BiFC; dual-luciferase
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI GEO/Sequence Read Archive BioProject PRJNA700690
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SIJIG is a MeJA-induced bHLH gene functioning downstream of COI1 and MYC2. 2. CRISPR knockout reduced expression of multiple TPS genes and lowered contents of 12 terpenes in leaves. 3. Loss of SIJIG increased attractiveness to Helicoverpa armigera and susceptibility to Botrytis cinerea. 4. SIJIG also affected induction of JA-responsive defense genes, linking terpene metabolism and JA defense output.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No transcriptional activation activity in yeast was detected; authors propose SIJIG may require unidentified partners to function.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related volatile terpenes involved in resistance to insects and fungus
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA-induced transcriptional activation of terpene biosynthesis through SIJIG
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master JA regulator that directly activates SIJIG, thereby enabling a MYC2-SIJIG module for terpene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional activation by promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SIJIG by binding an E-box-containing promoter fragment (p5)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: myc2 mutation or VIGS silencing strongly reduces JA-induced SIJIG expression, supporting positive control over terpene biosynthesis via SIJIG
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced rapidly by MeJA, peaking earlier than SIJIG
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SIJAZ1; SIJAZ2; SIJAZ3; SIJAZ4; SIJAZ5; SIJAZ6; SIJAZ7; SIJAZ8; SIJAZ9; SIJAZ10; SIJAZ11
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Part of canonical COI1/JAZ/MYC2 jasmonate signaling module
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts in a hierarchical transcriptional cascade
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SIJIG promoter fragments p1-p5 in Y1H; ~1 kb SIJIG promoter in dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream regulatory target for engineering jasmonate-inducible terpene-based defense, but broad JA pathway control may cause pleiotropic effects
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; CRISPR/Cas9 mutant; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; prior ChIP-seq support cited in paper
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq support cited from previous study; no new omics accession in this paper for MYC2 specifically
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC2 transcript induction precedes SIJIG induction after MeJA treatment. 2. Silencing or mutation of MYC2 lowers SIJIG expression and JA responsiveness. 3. MYC2 directly binds the SIJIG promoter and activates its transcription, defining a MYC2-SIJIG regulatory module.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the paper’s mechanistic model, MYC2 functions mainly as the upstream regulator of SIJIG rather than the primary newly identified gene.
